message: the same class of execution failure has recurred >=3x in the recent window without the approach changing — this is fable-mode's own trigger ("debugging where the first theory might be wrong", "a task keeps failing or stalling"). STOP retrying variations. Apply the five-gate loop: (1) restate the goal/scope, (2) re-gather evidence about WHY it fails (read the tool's contract, the actual state, the exact error), (3) adversarially falsify your current theory before the next attempt — assume the first theory is wrong and look for the disconfirming fact, (4) verify the fix fresh, (5) report. Load skills/fable-mode/SKILL.md if not already applied.
_note: H2a mechanical-evidence probe for the default-on fable-mode skill (the flagship auto-firing skill previously had SKILL.md only — description-triggered, zero mechanical firing signal; this closes that gap per the banked lesson 'make skills fire via mechanical probes, not description tuning'). DELIBERATELY complementary to compliance-canary's always-on trajectory_drift (which fires on error RATE errs/calls>=0.25 over >=8 calls): this fires on same-signature-class COUNT (>=3 matching errors) regardless of total call volume, so 3 recurring 'not found' errors inside 20 calls (rate 0.13, below trajectory_drift's floor) still surface. HONEST DEGRADATION (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3): a regex over tool-error text cannot detect fable-mode's OTHER trigger clauses ('a task has many layers / multiple dependent steps / unknowns that could change the approach') — those are semantic, not error-signature, phenomena and remain description-triggered only. This probe covers the failure/stall clause mechanically; the many-layers clause is accepted as description-only coverage, not full mechanical parity. ADVISORY heuristic: a broad error-word pattern can over-count unrelated errors (false-positive) or miss a paraphrased failure (false-negative); accepted per §3, scope-of-validity documented here.
